modeling_constraints: Do not hard-code validation stimulus, stop times, sample windows, gold internals, or simulator side channels.
problem_statement: the supplied system violates the public contract

properties.observable_contract: Measure analog inputs relative to the local `vss` rail and normalize by the current local supply span. Let `span = V(vdd, vss)` and treat the row as valid only when `V(en) > vth` and `span_min <= span <= span_max`; otherwise drive `out`, `flag`, and `metric` to `0 V`. If `span` is below `0.05 V`, use `0.05 V` as the normalization span. Define `clip01(y)` as `y` limited to the range `[0, 1]`, `x0..x3 = clip01((V(inN) - V(vss)) / span)`, and `c0 = clip01(V(ctrl0) / vhi)`.

Encode the highest-priority fault code using this order: if `x0 > 0.58`, use code `0.25`; else if `x1 > 0.58`, use code `0.50`; else if `x2 > 0.58`, use code `0.75`; else if `c0 > 0.58`, use code `1.0`; otherwise use code `0.0`. Drive `out = vhi * clip01(code)` and `metric = vhi * clip01(code)`. Assert `flag = vhi` when `code >= 0.50`, otherwise drive `flag = 0 V`.
Build a voltage-domain analog/mixed-signal helper or monitor. Priority fault encoder for supply, bias, reset, and mode faults using voltage-coded outputs.
`vth = 0.45 V`: logic threshold for voltage-coded controls.
`vhi = 0.9 V`: high level for output observables.
`span_min = 0.62 V`, `span_max = 1.28 V`: legal local supply span measured as

title: Priority Fault Code Driver
